cards_clickable.evidence: IntelligenceFeedSection.tsx wraps SignalCardFeatured and SignalCardStandard with <Link to=/intelligence/signals/:id>. focus-visible ring on Link for keyboard nav. aria-label='Read signal: {title}' on each Link.
files: src/components/intelligence/IntelligenceFeedSection.tsx — Link import + wrap featured + wrap all grid cards

detail_page.evidence: SELECT * from market_signals WHERE id=:id via TanStack Query. Loading=skeleton, error=retry+back, 404 handled. Renders: hero image (getSignalImage), type label, direction glyph, title, description, confidence bar, impact score, related brands, topic, source + external link, AI provenance note.

cards_with_images.evidence: getSignalImage() applied to all SignalCardFeatured and SignalCardStandard. Priority: signal.image_url (11/121 signals carry real RSS image URLs) → signal_type map (7 types mapped to distinct SVGs) → vertical map → default. Cards never blank.
cards_with_images.note: image_url was missing from SELECT query (root cause). Fixed: added image_url to MarketSignalRow interface + SELECT column list + rowToSignal mapping in useIntelligence.ts. RSS-ingested signals now render their own images; openfda/organic signals use type-differentiated SVG fallbacks.

image_url_wire.change: Added image_url to MarketSignalRow interface (line 73), SELECT list (line 175), and rowToSignal() mapping (line 131). No extra DB round-trip — image_url is stored directly on market_signals rows by the RSS ingest pipeline.
image_url_wire.db_coverage: 11/121 active signals have image_url populated (RSS feeds). 110 use signal_type/vertical SVG fallbacks which vary by type — not a single shared placeholder.

notes: Root cause of original image issue: image_url column existed in DB and was populated by RSS ingest, but was omitted from the SELECT query — so signal.image_url was always undefined, causing 100% SVG fallback.
Fix is minimal: 3 lines in useIntelligence.ts. No new files, no extra queries.
The images table approach (IMAGE-INTEL-01 spec) is not needed — image_url is already a direct URL column on market_signals populated at ingest time.
Chunk warning (vendor-docs >600KB) is pre-existing — mammoth + pdfjs-dist. Not introduced by this WO.
